name: Maize
description: Zambia's staple crop. Requires good rains early in the season. Proper basal and top dressing fertilizer application is critical for high yields.

name: Tomatoes
ideal_planting: Year-round (with irrigation), March-July for best prices
description: High value but highly perishable. Requires staking, regular irrigation, and strict pest/disease management (especially against Tuta absoluta and Blight).

name: Onions
ideal_planting: February to April (nursery), April to June (transplanting)
common_varieties: Texas Grano, Red Creole, Neptune F1
description: Requires well-prepared beds. Proper curing after harvest is essential for long shelf life to fetch better prices in the off-season.

name: Cabbage
ideal_planting: Year-round (with irrigation), best in cooler months (April-July)
common_varieties: Gloria F1, Marcanta F1, Fabiola F1
description: Heavy feeder requiring high nitrogen. Susceptible to Diamondback moth. Does exceptionally well heading into Zambia's cool dry season.

name: Irish Potatoes
soil_preference: Deep, loose, well-drained sandy loam, pH 5.0 - 6.0
description: Requires certified disease-free seed. High yields depend heavily on regular earthing up and strict Late Blight control.

name: Groundnuts
description: Drought tolerant. Needs calcium (Gypsum) at the pegging stage for proper pod filling. Harvest promptly to avoid aflatoxin contamination.

name: Soybeans
soil_preference: Well-drained loams or clay loams, pH 5.8 - 6.5
description: High commercial demand for oil and stock feed. Needs Rhizobium inoculant at planting for optimal nitrogen fixation.

name: Wheat
soil_preference: Silt or clay loam, well-drained, pH 6.0 - 7.0
spacing: Broadcasted or drilled at 20cm row spacing
description: Grown strictly under irrigation in Zambia during the cool dry season. Requires heavy capital investment in irrigation pivots.
